  "findings": [
    "Bend radius violations on data/communication cabling",
    "Kinked or over-bent low-voltage cables exceeding minimum bend radius limits"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Identify and replace damaged sections of data/communication cabling",
    "Re-route cables to maintain the minimum bend radius of 4 times the outer cable diameter for UTP (or per manufacturer specifications)",
    "Loosen over-tightened cable ties or supports causing localized pinching"
  ],
  "rationale": "The installation fails inspection because the data/communication cables have been bent beyond the allowable limits specified by TIA-568 and NEC Article 800. Exceeding the minimum bend radius can cause physical damage to the internal twisted pairs, leading to impedance mismatches, near-end crosstalk (NEXT), and failed performance testing.",
  "references": [
    "ANSI/TIA-568",
    "NEC Article 800"
  ]
}
